exemplar.title: Examining and comparing the correlation between the annual CO2 gas emissions and Gross Domestic Product (GDP)  in South Africa, an upper-middle-income African state, and Kenya, a lower-middle-income African state, from 2012 to 2022.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative structure with introduction, methods, results, evaluation and conclusion
Effective use of headings, sub-headings and table of contents for navigation
Graphics embedded close to related text enhance readability
weaknesses: Duplicated âEvaluationâ headings and minor misplacement weaken logical flow
Repetition of definitions and lengthy narrative inflate the report
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and generally well organized, with clear sections and logical progression. However, recurring digressions, duplicated headings and some repetition prevent it reaching the highest band. A score of 3 reflects its solid structure with room for improved conciseness and tighter organization.

strengths: Consistent use of standard symbols (Î£, xÌ, m, b) in most calculations
Use of multiple representations: tables, two scatter plots with regression lines and an optimization derivative
Logical sequence in derivations: formula, substitution, numerical result and interpretation
weaknesses: Axis labels on graphs often lack units and clear scales
Inconsistent notation in places (subscripts for COâ, reuse of Î¸ for zero)
Minor formula slip-ups (intercept formula, variable mix-ups) hinder clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly consistent and appropriate, and the student employs several representations effectively. Occasional notation errors and omissions hold the work back from full consistency. A band-3 score acknowledges mostly clear and organized communication with minor lapses.

strengths: Clear personal motivation in choice of home country and contrasting economy
Independent framing of a policy-relevant research question beyond standard exercises
weaknesses: Methods remain largely conventional with limited innovation
Optional optimization adds little fresh insight
Creativity and initiative are present but not outstanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates significant independent thinking and a personal approach, but the exploration follows familiar procedures with only modest creative extensions. A score of 2 reflects this clear engagement without reaching the highest level of innovation.

strengths: Meaningful reflective comments after each major method, questioning model assumptions
Thoughtful analysis of correlation results against the initial hypothesis
Clear evaluation of outcomes with policy implications and acknowledgment of limitations
weaknesses: Reflection is sometimes superficial and could probe deeper alternative explanations
Critical analysis of statistical validity (e.g., misuse of derivative) is limited
No discussion of confidence intervals or broader modeling choices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and thoughtfully linked to the investigation, but remains at a meaningful rather than substantial critical level. The student shows insight into method limitations and outcomes but stops short of the most comprehensive, forward-looking critique.

strengths: Appropriate and comprehensive use of linear regression, Pearsonâs r and interpolation
Correct understanding and application of y=mx+b and derivative concepts
Calculations are mostly correct and clearly presented
weaknesses: Coefficient of determination (RÂ²) is not calculated to quantify model fit
Labeling the derivative of a linear function as an optimization exercise is misleading
Minor errors in derivative notation and intercept computation do not undermine overall validity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is fully commensurate with SL level and applied effectively across regression, correlation, interpolation and basic calculus. While deeper model-fit metrics and a non-linear optimization would strengthen the work, the scope and accuracy justify a band-4 score.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations usually follow a logical sequence (formula â substitution â numeric answer â interpretation).  Yet occasional slips (wrong variable labels, b-formula error for South Africa, derivative notation mix-up) hinder clarity.  Logical structure is present but not consistently clear, placing it in band 2.

Calculations are mostly correct and clearly shown; minor slips (incorrect b-value formula, derivative notation) do not greatly affect conclusions.  Interpretations of r and RÂ² are sound.  Understanding is good with minor errors, matching band 3.

The student employs tables of summary statistics, two scatter plots with regression line, and a brief optimisation derivative.  However axes on graphs lack units, scales are coarse, and no residual plot or additional representation is provided.  Hence âsomeâ but not âeffectiveâ multiple representations.

The student follows a clear narrative (introduction â method â results â evaluation â conclusion).  Headings, sub-headings and sequential explanations make the argument easy to follow and most paragraphs link logically.  Nonetheless, occasional digressions (history of industrialisation, definition blocks repeated) interrupt the flow, and several evaluations are placed mid-analysis, slightly weakening logical progression.  Overall coherence is good but not exemplary, hence band 3 rather than 4.

Apart from including an optional optimisation attempt (which adds no new insight) the exploration follows routine statistical procedures.  Limited creativity or initiative is evident, so the lowest positive band (1) is most appropriate.

The student contrasts r-values, challenges initial hypothesis, and relates findings to industrial maturity, demonstrating thoughtful analysis.  Nevertheless, statistical validity (e.g., misuse of derivative for optimisation) is not deeply critiqued.  Thus analysis is âthoughtfulâ but not âdeep and insightfulâ: band 2.

Outcomes are compared against predictions and practical implications for policy are briefly discussed.  Limitations and future improvements are acknowledged.  Evaluation is clear but not comprehensive (no statistical uncertainty, no alternate models examined).  Band 2 best matches the evidence.

Reflective comments appear after each major method: the student questions intercept meaning, acknowledges model weakness, and notes data limitations.  Reflection is present throughout but brief and sometimes superficial, aligning with âmeaningful reflectionâ (band 2) rather than âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Correct mathematical symbols (Î£, xÌ, r, RÂ²) are used, but with frequent inconsistencies:  âÎ£xÂ·10â»Â²â, âÎ¸â instead of 0, âÎ±â in prose, units missing or mixed.  Subscripts on COâ sometimes appear as âCO<sub>2</sub>â and elsewhere âCO_2â.  Thus appropriate language appears but not consistently, warranting band 2.

The narrative is framed around the studentâs personal question and policy motivation, and explanations are written in first-person, indicating a clear personal approach.  However the approach is not particularly innovative; it mirrors standard IA exemplars.  Band 2 is the best fit.

The exploration is generally relevant, but stretches to 20 pages for a modest statistical task.  Several paragraphs repeat earlier statements (e.g. repeated explanation of regression; three separate âEvaluationâ sub-sections) and long narrative sentences inflate length.  Consequently the work is mostly relevant with some unnecessary elements, fitting band 2.

Simple linear regression, Pearson r, interpolation and an elementary derivative are all relevant to the stated question.  No extraneous mathematics is included, so relevance is appropriate but the work remains limited in scope; ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3) fits.

The choice of the studentâs home country and a contrasting economy shows personal interest, and the decision to test a preconceived assumption demonstrates independent thinking beyond a textbook exercise.  Nevertheless, mathematical treatment remains conventional; there is limited originality in method.  Evidence therefore matches âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

The report is divided into Introduction, Aim, Data, several method sections, three rounds of Evaluation, and a final Conclusion.  A table of contents with page numbers is supplied and most sections appear in the announced order.  Graphics are embedded close to the discussion.  Minor organisational weaknesses (duplicated âEvaluationâ headings, incorrect table titles, misplaced optimisation section) prevent it reaching the very highest descriptor.

Content sits squarely within the AA-SL syllabus (topic 4 statistics, basic calculus).  There is no material either below or above level.  Application is competent but not extensive, so band 3 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The duplicated âEvaluationâ entries in the table of contents interrupt the logical flow of the report. Consolidate evaluation sections under a single heading or clearly differentiate their purposes to improve coherence and organization.

The introductionâs historical overview of Africaâs industrialization is interesting but digresses from the focused research question. Trim background material to maintain relevance and conciseness.
The studentâs link to a personal school discussion and choice of home country demonstrates clear personal engagement and independent thinking in framing the investigation.
The rationale section could deepen critical reflection by discussing alternate explanations (e.g., energy policy or population growth) that might influence COâ emissions independently of GDP.
The data collection description lacks a full citation for the UN Environment Programme data (e.g., retrieval date and URL). Provide complete source details to enhance credibility and presentation.
An âEvaluationâ heading appears in the middle of analysis on this page, interrupting progression. Consider grouping all evaluations at the end of sections for better logical development.
The limitation acknowledges the small sample of two states, demonstrating critical awareness. Expanding the dataset would strengthen the investigationâs generalizability.
Notation for the dependent variable switches between v and y inconsistently. Standardize on y for COâ emissions throughout to improve mathematical clarity.
The equation \(y=mx+b\) is correctly stated and provides a clear foundation for the regression analysis.
The regression data table for Kenya uses varying decimal precision and omits units for sums (e.g., âx, ây). Consistent rounding and unit labels will enhance readability.
The intercept formula \(b=\overline y - m\overline x\) is correctly applied, showing thorough understanding of regression line derivation.
Your interpretation of the small slope as a weak relationship is insightful, but the coefficient of determination (RÂ²) should be calculated and presented here to quantify the modelâs fit.
Phrases such as âI noticedâ introduce informal tone. Adopt an objective narrative (e.g., âIt was observedâ) to maintain professional mathematical communication.
The scatter plot lacks axis labels with units and clearly marked scales. Including these will significantly improve clarity and interpretation of the data.
The discussion of the scatter plot lacks reference to the Pearson r value later calculated. Linking visual and quantitative analyses would strengthen the evaluation.
The header âxÂ·10â»Â² appears to be a typo instead of âxÂ². Correct this notation to avoid confusion in the regression calculation.
The intercept formula in this block omits the factor m for xÌ. It should read b= yÌ â m xÌ to correctly compute the y-intercept.
The text attributes Kenyaâs slope to South Africa in this discussion. Ensure each countryâs analysis is clearly labeled to avoid misinterpretation.
The section repeats Kenya when it should discuss South Africa. Copyâpaste errors here disrupt clarityârevise to accurately reflect each country.
The wording âcarbon populationâ is unclear. Use precise terminology such as âmass of carbon emissionsâ to maintain mathematical accuracy.
The interpretation table for râvalue ranges is clear and effectively supports the subsequent analysis of correlation strength.
The use of the Greek letter Î¸ to denote zero correlation is unconventional. Replace Î¸ with 0 to avoid confusion.
In the evaluation you introduce bold mathematics and switch between Î± and a. Maintain consistent, standard formatting of variables without bold styling.
The discussion critically contrasts results against the initial hypothesis, demonstrating meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of the investigationâs implications.
The interpolation method is well justified but would benefit from discussion of confidence intervals around predicted values to assess their reliability.
The interpolation block sets y=113.42 instead of x=113.42, resulting in a fundamental variable misplacement; correct the substitution for accurate prediction.
Labeling the constant derivative of a linear function as an optimization exercise is misleading; a nonâlinear model is required to find a meaningful critical point.
The interpretation correctly notes the absence of a stationary point but would be enriched by discussing economic and policy implications of the alwaysâincreasing trend.
The conclusion introduces new sources (New York Times, Worldometers) without consistent citation formatting. Standardize references in a recognized style.
